primary_topic: Welding of titanium: processes, Soviet technology transfer, alloy metallurgy, shielding requirements
principal_cases: Gurevich and Soviet titanium welding research (electroslag, semi-submerged GTA)
Submerged arc welding of titanium at MIT (1970s, calcium fluoride flux)
Alvin and Sea Cliff deep-submergence vehicles
Draper Lab four-foot titanium submarine project (early 1990s)
TIMET and the economics of small-market alloy mills
Naval Air Rework Facility titanium compressor case repair (1969)
people_mentioned: Gurevich (Soviet titanium welding expert, Paton Institute)
B. I. Medovar (Soviet electroslag welding pioneer)
Gordon (Tom's student, helicopter rotor weld repair, ~1980s)
institutions_mentioned: US Navy / David Taylor Ship Research Center
materials_systems: Higher-strength sheet titanium alloys (180-220 ksi range)
Calcium fluoride / strontium fluoride welding fluxes
era_anchors: 1980 (Alpha sub revealed; Tom visits Soviet Union)
notes: Section is a single continuous teaching unit on titanium welding, opening with admin remarks and Gurevich/KGB anecdote, then moving through welding processes, alpha-case metallurgy, market economics, and naval applications. Several captioner garbles flagged for editorial review (see transformation_log.md). One probable Tom misspeak (world steel production 'a million tons' → 'a billion tons') silently corrected because adjacent arithmetic confirms intent.
